title: prunella laciniata subsp. subintegra
intro: Prunella laciniata subsp. subintegra, also known as Selfheal, is a subspecies of plant in the Lamiaceae family, characterized by its small, purple flowers and heart-shaped leaves. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which are attributed to its bioactive compounds such as flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Selfheal include its ability to boost the immune system, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antioxidant properties, making it a popular remedy for colds, fever, and digestive issues. Selfheal can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to support overall well-being and alleviate various health concerns.
